description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from dark, weathered wooden staves. The barrel is reinforced by three wide, rusted metal hoops that encircle its body horizontally, providing both structural integrity and a rustic appearance. The wood grain is visible and uneven, with some areas appearing more worn and aged than others, suggesting long-term outdoor exposure. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same dark wood, with no visible openings or spouts. There are no handles or additional features, and the surface shows a matte, rough finish with patches of discoloration and rust on the metal bands. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban alleys, storage yards, or as decorative elements in parks and public spaces.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with rusted metal hoops and a rough, matte finish, showing signs of age and outdoor use.
description_view_0: The barrel's front view shows the full curvature of the staves and the prominent rusted metal hoops, with the wood grain and aging most visible.
description_view_1: This view highlights the same features as the front, but the lighting accentuates the texture of the wood and the unevenness of the metal bands.
description_view_2: The barrel appears slightly darker on this side, with the wood grain and rusted hoops still clearly visible, and no additional features.
description_view_3: This view shows a similar profile to the others, with the curvature of the barrel and the weathered texture of both wood and metal bands.
